feat(watchdog): detect escalated AC runs and auto-trigger skill fixes

- scan_escalated_ac_runs(): scans ac_project_state for recent escalations (last 2h),
  maps reason patterns to specific skill files (ac-codex.md, ac-architect.md),
  triggers quality-improvement with targeted brief + skill_hints
- _ESCALATION_SKILL_MAP: 5 patterns → fake tool calls, XXX markers, too short,
  no tests, hallucination → skill file + fix description
- _launch_quality_improvement(): now accepts skill_hints for precise briefs
- watchdog_cycle(): includes scan_escalated_ac_runs() in scan pipeline
- workflows.py: stores last_escalation_reason + last_escalation_at in
  ac_project_state when cycle escalates (extracted from phase_results)
- pages.py: adds last_escalation_reason/at columns in migrations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add buffer/overflow safety checks across data layer (#1215)

- WalSegmentReader: validate compressedLen before cast to int, use checked arithmetic
- WalStore: add MaxPayloadSize (100MB) limit before .ToArray(), validate compressedLen overflow
- IndexStore: add bounds checks before span slicing in TryParseRawEntry, TryParseSnapshotLine, TryParseRegistryEntry
- SynchronousEncryption: use checked() for payload buffer size calculation
- BinaryObjectSerializer: add bounds checks before every blittable read/write
- BmwJsonReader: validate string length limit and use checked() in ParseStringValue
- WalDataProvider: validate entryCount against actual file size in LoadIdMapCore

Closes #1153, closes #1154, closes #1155, closes #1156, closes #1157, closes #1158, closes #1159

Co-authored-by: WillEastbury <WillEastbury@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: auto-deploy oauth-proxy on push to main (#9)

Triggers on changes to oauth-proxy/, infra/, or the workflow itself.
Push-triggered runs deploy to staging only; production requires
manual workflow_dispatch with approval gate.

Co-authored-by: Brooke Philpott <bphilpott@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add step-by-step OIDC federation setup for GitHub Actions (#8)

Replaces bare secret table with full walkthrough: app registration,
federated credential, role assignments, and GitHub secrets/variables.

Co-authored-by: Brooke Philpott <bphilpott@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add cross-validation check for symlinks.toml/manifest.toml section drift (#273)

* Initial plan

* Add ValidateManifestSync task and config_drift reverse test

- Add ValidateManifestSync task in tasks/validation.rs that loads raw
  section keys from both conf/symlinks.toml and conf/manifest.toml,
  checks both directions of drift (excluding [base] from symlinks),
  and logs errors + bails when sections are out of sync.
- Wire ValidateManifestSync into commands/test.rs so it runs as part
  of `dotfiles test`.
- Add manifest_sections_have_symlink_sections integration test to
  config_drift.rs checking the reverse direction (manifest keys not in
  symlinks.toml).
- Add contributor comments to conf/symlinks.toml and conf/manifest.toml
  explaining the sync requirement and pointing to `dotfiles test`.

Co-authored-by: sneivandt <8406046+sneivandt@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sneivandt <8406046+sneivandt@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: agent_auto planner reads current page URL instead of defaulting to about:blank

The task planner always generated 'navigate → about:blank' as step 0 when
the goal text didn't contain an explicit URL. Now agent_auto_run() reads
the current page URL via page.url() and injects it into the planning
context, so the navigate step targets the actual page.

- Added plan_with_url() accepting an optional current URL
- agent_auto_run() now passes the live page URL to the planner
- plan() remains backward-compatible (dry-run without page access)
- Added 2 unit tests verifying URL injection and about:blank fallback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover cleanup guard and filter callback in useBgImage and useTauriStore (#1225)

useBgImage.test.ts:
- Add test rendering useBackgroundImage + useBackgroundImageList under the
  same Provider to cover the filter callback (v) => v.fileId !== fileId at
  line 68 of useBgImage.ts; previously backgroundImageList was always empty
  in the deleteBackgroundImage context because separate Providers were used
- Import waitFor from @testing-library/react for the new assertion

useTauriStore.test.ts:
- Add test that mounts then immediately unmounts the hook before the async
  store fetch resolves; covers the useEffect cleanup (line 40:
  isMountedRef.current = false) and the false branch of the mounted guard
  (line 31: if (isMountedRef.current)) which prevents setState after unmount

Coverage impact (all files):
  Functions: 95.21% -> 96.01%
  Branches:  92.09% -> 92.37%
  Statements: 97.57% -> 97.76%
  Lines: 98.41% -> 98.61%

useBgImage.ts: 88.88% -> 100% functions, 100% branches maintained
useTauriStore.ts: 80% -> 100% functions, 90% -> 100% branches

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: shm <m11c3.sh@gmail.com>

commit.message:
masternode: Direct TCP connection is authoritative for is_active, gossip is secondary

cleanup_stale_reports was unconditionally overwriting is_active with a
gossip-report count even when the node had a live TCP connection. This caused
OnChain-registered masternodes to flip to inactive every 60s if they had fewer
than min_reports (2-3) gossip witnesses — which happens with any node that has
only 1-2 connections (common in small testnets and pyramid leaf nodes).

Fix: snapshot connected peers at the start of each cleanup cycle and skip the
gossip-based flip for any masternode that is directly connected. Gossip still
controls is_active for nodes we have no direct connection to (the normal
Sybil-resistance case). Pass peer_registry into start_report_cleanup and
cleanup_stale_reports for this check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: time-masternode

commit.message:
perf: Add noclone to DSL_NOINLINE, restore blend_over noinline

Add __attribute__((noclone)) alongside noinline in the DSL_NOINLINE
macro.  GCC 14 was creating constprop clones of noise2, noise3, and
blend_over despite noinline, causing register pressure and I-cache
thrash on the ESP32's 32KB I-cache.

Also restores DSL_NOINLINE on dsl_blend_over() (was changed to inline
in 54ce7b9), which prevented the macro from taking effect.

forest-wind results on hardware:
  Before: 89.9 ms display, 10.9 FPS
  After:  34.2 ms display, 27.9 FPS (2.6× faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: sync §4.1 users table with live schema (email, password_hash, refresh_tokens)

- Add email VARCHAR_254 UK and password_hash VARCHAR_72 to users table —
  both are non-nullable required columns present in the Prisma schema
  that were missing from the ER diagram
- Add UK annotation on username (already @unique in schema)
- Add refresh_tokens table (id, token_hash UK, user_id FK, expires_at,
  revoked_at, created_at) which exists in the live schema but was absent
  from the spec entirely
- Add users ||--o{ refresh_tokens relationship to the ER diagram

test: Add comprehensive unit tests and quality checks

Implement comprehensive testing and code quality configuration:

Test Framework:
- JUnit5 (Jupiter) for modern testing capabilities
- Mockito for mocking and interaction verification
- 40 unit tests across 5 test classes
- ~93% average code coverage

Test Classes:
- CartServiceTest: 13 tests for CRUD operations, events, metrics
- CartControllerTest: 11 tests for REST endpoints, validation, errors
- CartEventPublisherTest: 8 tests for Kafka event publishing
- DailyCartStatsSchedulerTest: 3 tests for scheduled job logic
- HealthIndicatorsTest: 5 tests for health check implementations

Code Quality Tools:
- Checkstyle plugin: Google Java Style compliance
- PMD plugin: Static code analysis and best practices
- JaCoCo plugin: Code coverage reporting
- Surefire plugin: Test execution and reporting

Configuration:
- Updated pom.xml with testing dependencies
- Added maven plugins for quality gates
- Test includes: Service, Controller, Events, Scheduler, Health
- Mocking: Spring's MockMvc, Mockito annotations, ArgumentCaptor

Test Coverage:
- CartService: ~95% coverage
- CartController: ~90% coverage
- Event publishing: ~95% coverage
- Scheduler: ~90% coverage
- Health indicators: ~95% coverage

Quality Metrics:
- All tests use proper naming and display names
- Comprehensive error scenario testing
- Input validation verification
- Mock interaction verification

refactor(seed): replace env mutation with explicit flag, add conflict tests, move seed entry

  allowMockSeed=false flag on seedMockData(); demoSeed passes true
  directly so no global env is mutated (addresses review comment)
- Remove redundant main() from mockSeed.ts; add prisma/seed.ts as the
  canonical Prisma-convention entry point for 'prisma db seed', reducing
  drift risk between the two seed paths (addresses review comments)
- Update package.json prisma.seed to point to prisma/seed.ts
- Export assertNoUniqueConflicts and RawSnapshot; add four focused tests
  covering empty DB, matching UUIDs, username conflict, and email
  conflict with a mocked PrismaClient (addresses review comment)
- Add TOCTOU comment on assertNoUniqueConflicts call in seedMockData
  documenting the intentional pre-flight / non-atomic trade-off
- Remove enableDemoSeed from demo-seed.test.ts (function removed)

commit.message:
Fix MCP server binding to 0.0.0.0 for container deployments

FastMCP SSE transport defaults to 127.0.0.1 which is inaccessible
from outside the container. Explicitly bind to 0.0.0.0 so ACI/Docker
can route external traffic to the server.

ci: add fuzz corpus sanitizer scan workflow

3-job workflow that cross-repo checks out xsscx/fuzz corpus:
1. build-tools: Build iccanalyzer-lite + IccToXml/IccFromXml with ASAN+UBSAN
2. scan-icc-profiles: Run 95 ICC profiles through analyzer + IccToXml
3. scan-icc-xml: Run 116 XML files through IccFromXml + round-trip validation

Artifacts: per-file ASAN/UBSAN reports, coverage data, markdown summaries
Triggers: push to iccanalyzer-lite/** or cfl/patches/**, workflow_dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add integration tests for protocol stack

Implements end-to-end integration tests covering the protocol → rasterizer
→ display pipeline. This addresses audit finding AUDIT-2026-03-07-v3.md
'No integration tests for protocol stack'.

Changes:
- Created internal/integration package with comprehensive integration tests
- TestWaylandClientStack: Validates end-to-end Wayland client with mock
  compositor, testing wl_display.sync request/response cycle
- TestProtocolRasterDisplayPipeline: Verifies wire protocol encoding,
  rasterizer buffer creation, and display preparation
- TestX11ProtocolIntegration: Validates X11 CreateWindow request encoding
- BenchmarkWireEncoding: Performance benchmark for wire protocol encoding

Tests validate component integration without requiring a running compositor
or X server, catching integration bugs that unit tests miss (event sequencing,
buffer lifecycle, fd leakage).

All tests pass (20 packages). Zero regressions in complexity metrics.

Resolves: Audit finding - No integration tests for protocol stack

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace uint.Parse with TryParse on user input in API handlers (#1212)

Replace all uint.Parse calls on user-supplied route parameters, query
strings, and cookie values in the Host project with uint.TryParse and
proper 400 Bad Request error responses. This prevents unhandled
FormatException/OverflowException when clients send non-numeric IDs.

Closes #1181

commit.message:
feat: add mock Kubernetes server to Dev Container (#1568)

- [x] Explore repository structure and CI validation workflow
- [x] Install `kind` and `kubectl` in `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` with
renovate-tracked versions
- [x] Add checksum verification for `kind` and `kubectl` downloads
(consistent `awk '{print $1}'` pattern for both)
- [x] Create `.devcontainer/setup-mock-k8s.sh` — idempotent script that
creates the `mock` kind cluster and installs GKE CRDs
- [x] Fix POSIX compatibility: replace `&>` with `>/dev/null 2>&1` in
the `sh -c` wait loop
- [x] Add `kind export kubeconfig` before `kubectl apply` to guarantee
correct cluster context whether cluster is new or pre-existing
- [x] Update `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` to run setup script via
`postCreateCommand`
- [x] Add documentation `docs/dev-container-mock-k8s.md` with
instructions for using the mock server

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Use mock k8s server in Dev Container</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> 
> Add mock Kubernetes server support to the Dev Container environment to
enable local server-side manifest validation, similar to the CI
validation workflow.
> 
> ## Context
>

commit.message:
fix: set productVersion in wails.json during CI build

Homebrew cask upgrades require the app bundle's Info.plist version
(CFBundleShortVersionString) to match the cask version. Without this,
the hardcoded productVersion in wails.json causes brew upgrade --cask
to fail because the installed app always reports the old version.
